patient_information.patient_address: 45 E Parkway Ave, New York, NY 21022

physician_contact.office_address: 36 E Ridgeview St, New York, NY 21023

clinical_information.prior_treatments_and_results: Topical triamcinolone cream up to twice daily and regular use of emollients (Aquaphor) for approximately 3 weeks without significant relief; persistent pruritic dry patches.
clinical_information.symptom_severity_and_impact: SCORAD 35 (moderate to severe). Dry, flaky skin with excoriation; erythematous pruritic plaques on flexural areas (elbows, knees) and face. Pruritus affects ability to sleep and lesions occasionally bleed when scratched.
clinical_information.prognosis_and_risk_if_not_approved: Without approval of systemic therapy, patient is likely to have persistent uncontrolled atopic dermatitis with ongoing sleep disturbance, continued skin excoriation and bleeding, and impaired quality of life; may require escalation of care if symptoms remain uncontrolled.
clinical_information.clinical_rationale_for_urgency: Patient has moderate-to-severe disease (SCORAD 35) with poor response to topical therapy over 3 weeks and clinically significant impact on sleep and skin integrity; provider recommends initiating dupilumab (systemic therapy) for better symptom control with specified initial and maintenance dosing.

treatment_request.dosage: 300mg initial dose (divided as two 150mg injections), followed by 300mg every 2 weeks
treatment_request.rationale: Severe atopic dermatitis; inadequate response to Triamcinolone 0.5% ointment after a 3 week trial
